_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use words we have picked up from everyday life, from things we are interested in, and from what we study at school.

_source.w: We are learning to use words we have picked up from everyday life, from things we are interested in, and from what we study at school.
y: A bigger vocabulary means we can talk about more things more clearly.
Using words from our interests makes conversations more fun and personal.
Learning topic words helps us understand and remember what we study.
eg: We recognise and respond to frequently used words in the classroom, like 'pack up' or 'circle time'.
We use words from our science topic, like 'habitat' or 'camouflage', in conversation.
We share words connected to our hobbies — like 'goal', 'score' or 'dribble' for football.
We add new words we learn to a personal word wall or dictionary.
We play vocabulary games where we match new words to their meanings.
We try to use at least one new word we learned this week in our speaking or writing.
